subparts.text: Write a short note on Corporate Social Responsibility.
“The Trusteeship Principle exists only in theory but not in practice.” Critique in the context of corporate governance.
Explain key provisions of Companies Act 2013 and revised Clause 49 of Listing Agreement about corporate governance in India.
Analyse the foundation of corporate governance across globe using examples of various global committees.
‘Two-tier board companies are better governed than one-tier board companies.’ Explain with suitable illustrations.
What is executive and managerial compensation. How it differs from other forms of compensation.
Do ethics and governance go hand in hand? Explain in the context of Wipro.
“Wipro’s ethical winning spree is not free from its set of issues and challenges.” Explain.

questions.question_text: Explain different approaches to CSR which are followed by corporate houses. Which CSR model is considered supreme?

subparts.text: What are the outcomes of Supreme Court ruling for stakeholder’s interests at large?
What lessons are learnt about corporate governance from the present case?

questions.question_text: “Whistle-blowing has drawn attention to various corporate frauds in recent times but at the same time has been abusive.” Critique. Discuss the merits and demerits of whistle-blowing.
Illustrate and contrast the corporate governance system in USA and any other country of your choice. Also, explain the provisions of Sarbanes Oxley Act .

subparts.text: What could Nissan have done differently to avoid its downfall in the 1990s?
What circumstances made Ghosn’s misconduct possible? How can companies prevent such misconduct?
Critically evaluate the corporate governance practices adopted by Nissan
Roles and Responsibilities of Board of Directors
Duties entrusted on the Board of Directors

questions.question_text: “Satyam is called as the Enron of East.” Critique. Who are the real culprits of Satyam fiasco? Is it only the promoters or others as well?
